articles.title: Robotic-surgery Intuitive promotes Taylor Patton to commercial marketing chief
articles.summary: Intuitive (NASDAQ: ISRG) has appointed Taylor Patton as its new Chief Commercial and Marketing Officer, effective July 1, 2026. Patton, who has nearly two decades of experience with Intuitive, succeeds Henry Charlton, who will transition to Senior Vice President of Global Business Operations. This change emphasizes Intuitive's ongoing strategy to scale global operations and enhance customer support for its robotic-assisted surgery platforms.
articles.content: Intuitive (NASDAQ: ISRG) has appointed Taylor Patton as its new Chief Commercial and Marketing Officer, effective July 1, 2026. Patton, who has nearly two decades of experience with Intuitive, succeeds Henry Charlton, who will transition to Senior Vice President of Global Business Operations. This change emphasizes Intuitive's ongoing strategy to scale global operations and enhance customer support for its robotic-assisted surgery platforms.

articles.title: Rosen Law Firm Urges Zoetis Inc. (NYSE: ZTS) Stockholders to Contact the Firm for Information About Their Rights
articles.summary: Rosen Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Zoetis Inc. (NYSE: ZTS) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between January 14, 2025, and May 6, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Zoetis made false or misleading statements regarding its business operations, specifically concerning weakening sales and market share losses for key companion animal products. Stockholders are urged to contact the firm by July 27, 2026, to potentially serve as lead plaintiff.
articles.content: Rosen Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Zoetis Inc. (NYSE: ZTS) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between January 14, 2025, and May 6, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Zoetis made false or misleading statements regarding its business operations, specifically concerning weakening sales and market share losses for key companion animal products. Stockholders are urged to contact the firm by July 27, 2026, to potentially serve as lead plaintiff.

articles.title: Clorox CEO Linda Rendle to step down for health reasons
articles.summary: Clorox CEO Linda Rendle will step down from her role for health reasons after six years as CEO and over two decades with the company. The Board of Directors will initiate a comprehensive search for her successor, with Rendle remaining in her position during the transition and serving in an advisory capacity afterward. This announcement comes as Clorox shares have declined, although the company maintains financial stability with a strong dividend yield and a 49-year history of dividend increases.
articles.content: Clorox CEO Linda Rendle will step down from her role for health reasons after six years as CEO and over two decades with the company. The Board of Directors will initiate a comprehensive search for her successor, with Rendle remaining in her position during the transition and serving in an advisory capacity afterward. This announcement comes as Clorox shares have declined, although the company maintains financial stability with a strong dividend yield and a 49-year history of dividend increases.

articles.title: Pentair stock hits 52-week low at 70.69 USD By Investing.com
articles.summary: Pentair stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $70.87, trading just above its lowest point of $70.73. Despite the significant 28.76% decline over the past year and over 30% year-to-date, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, and the company has consistently maintained its dividend for over 51 years. Pentair recently announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.27 per share, and its Flow segment showed strong Q1 performance, though analysts like TD Cowen have lowered price targets due to concerns about the pool market outlook.
